era: Seattle, 2010s. Electronic/dance duo. High-energy, synth-driven dance music with punk ethos. Self-released. Confrontational, DIY.

location.formative: Seattle electronic scene. Punk-dance intersection. DIY culture. Self-released. Dance floor as performance art.
location.career: Hecuba -- Dance/Electronic. Seattle duo. High-energy, synth-driven dance music. Self-released. E=5.

associated_musicians.name: ODESZA
associated_musicians.note: Both E=5 electronic. ODESZA is cinematic, festival-scale. Hecuba is confrontational, dance-floor-scale.

associated_musicians.name: STRFKR
associated_musicians.note: Both E=5, both synth-driven, both punk energy. STRFKR is joyful. Hecuba is confrontational.

associated_musicians.name: Hovercraft
associated_musicians.note: Both aggressive electronic. Hovercraft is noise-electronic. Hecuba is dance-electronic. Different targets.

associated_musicians.name: Mates of State
associated_musicians.note: Both E=5 duos. Mates of State channels exuberance through indie pop. Hecuba through dance provocation.

associated_musicians.name: Erik Blood
associated_musicians.note: Both experimental electronic from Seattle. Erik Blood is atmospheric. Hecuba is physical. Different directions.

theory_nodes.concept: The kinetic surge (one hundred and second acoustic element -- high-energy dance music as physical force; pounding beats, distorted synths, shouted vocals, relentless forward momentum; energy as texture; the body moves; not flowing warmth but propulsive heat; punk attitude fused with dance beats)
examples: Hecuba (degree 101) -- pounding beats, distorted synths, shouted vocals. E=5. 102nd acoustic element.
Contrast with the nostalgic cascade (degree 100, Summer Heart) -- flowing warmth. The kinetic surge is propulsive heat.
Contrast with the bilingual warmth (degree 99, Kaina) -- cultural fusion. The kinetic surge is physical fusion (punk + dance).
One hundred and two acoustic elements at the first post-centennial degree.
theory_nodes.description: The kinetic surge is the 102nd acoustic element. High-energy dance music as physical force. Pounding beats. Energy as texture. The body moves.

ancestors: The nostalgic cascade (degree 100, Summer Heart) -- flowing warmth. The kinetic surge is propulsive heat.
The euphoric build (degree 95, ODESZA) -- cinematic escalation. The kinetic surge is flat-out relentless.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred and two acoustic elements across one hundred and one degrees.

theory_nodes.concept: The dance floor provocateur (one hundred and second artist-city pattern -- duo treating the dance floor as confrontation and performance art; punk ethos in electronic form; self-released, DIY; Seattle's punk history channeled through synths and beats; the floor as arena)
examples: Hecuba (degree 101) -- Seattle duo, self-released, confrontational dance music. 102nd artist-city pattern.
Contrast with the transatlantic dreamer (degree 100, Summer Heart) -- warm international bridge. The provocateur is local and physical.
Electronic-adjacent pattern lineage: ODESZA (95), STRFKR (96), Gold Casio (98), Kaina (99), Summer Heart (100), Hecuba (101).
theory_nodes.description: The dance floor provocateur is the 102nd artist-city pattern. Punk meets beat. Dance floor as confrontation. Self-released duo.

ancestors: The transatlantic dreamer (degree 100, Summer Heart) -- warm international. The provocateur is local and physical.
The self-released synthesist (degree 98, Gold Casio) -- self-released but warm. The provocateur is self-released but raw.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred and two artist-city patterns across one hundred and one degrees.

theory_nodes.concept: Dance/Electronic at Genre Stage 83 -- new genre classification; first explicitly dance-oriented genre in the engine; beat-driven, physical, designed for the floor; distinct from Electronic Pop, Indie Electronic, Electronic/R&B
examples: First explicitly dance-oriented genre. Beat over melody. Punk-influenced. Physical.
Distinct from Electronic Pop (100), Indie Electronic (96-98), Electronic/R&B (99).
theory_nodes.description: Genre Stage 83. Dance/Electronic. First dance-oriented genre. Beat-driven. Physical. Punk-influenced.

ancestors: Electronic Pop at Genre Stage 82 (degree 100, Summer Heart). The genre differentiates further at 101.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 83 at degree 101. Track at 102.

theory_nodes.concept: Post-centennial at E=5 -- first degree after the centennial; E=5 returns after E=4 streak (97-100); the electronic era accelerates; maximum energy; twelfth electronic-adjacent entry in sixteen degrees
examples: Degree 101 -- first after centennial. E=5 returns. Maximum energy.
E=4 streak broken: 97 (E=4), 98 (E=4), 99 (E=4), 100 (E=4), 101 (E=5). The engine accelerates.
theory_nodes.description: Post-centennial. E=5 returns. The electronic era continues. Twelfth electronic-adjacent entry. Maximum energy.

ancestors: The centennial at degree 100. The engine does not rest -- it accelerates.

cross_references.relationship: E=5 ELECTRONIC ENERGY. Both E=5 electronic. ODESZA is cinematic, festival-scale, brass-accented. Hecuba is confrontational, dance-floor-scale, punk-inflected. Different architectures of maximum energy.
E=5 ELECTRONIC ENERGY. Both E=5, both synth-driven, both punk energy. STRFKR is joyful and driving. Hecuba is confrontational and pounding. Different attitudes at maximum energy.
ELECTRONIC SPECTRUM. Both aggressive electronic. Hovercraft is noise-electronic, abstract. Hecuba is dance-electronic, beat-driven. Both confrontational; different targets.
E=5 EXUBERANCE. Both E=5, both duos. Mates of State channels exuberance through indie pop. Hecuba through dance provocation. Same voltage, different genres.
EXPERIMENTAL ELECTRONIC. Both experimental electronic from Seattle. Erik Blood is atmospheric and layered. Hecuba is raw and physical. Different directions of experimentation.
